key_insight: A microstructure-conditioned hybrid surrogate, implemented with a hypernetwork, can predict multiscale behavior across different mycelium composite microstructures with far less data than fixed-microstructure surrogates.
engineering_relevance: This makes graded multiscale optimization of sustainable, microarchitected materials computationally tractable and enables design of structures with lower stress concentrations.
method: Hybrid physics-data surrogate for microscale response
Conditioning on microstructural variables via a hypernetwork
Extended to manufacturing variables to map process-to-microstructure-to-performance
evidence_signal: The conditioned surrogate was validated against full FE^2 simulation and used to optimize a graded multiscale disk, reducing peak stress by 42% versus a random-microstructure design.
watch_item: Performance depends on the representativeness of the small training set, so extrapolation to unseen microstructures or manufacturing conditions may still be unreliable.
paper_title: Microstructure-Conditioned Surrogate Models for Graded Multiscale Optimization of Mycelium Composites
